questions.scenario: A quick Cloud Run test for `devpolaris-orders-api` now handles production-like traffic in a personal learning project. What is the safest first correction?

options.text: Create or select an owned production project with a clear folder, billing account, API set, and labels.
Keep production in the personal project because the service is already running.
Move only the display name, because display names move resources.
Disable all inherited policy so the deployment is easier.

questions.explanation: Production needs an intentional project boundary. The project should be reviewable before the quick test becomes accidental infrastructure.

questions.scenario: The team wants separate dev, staging, and production blast-radius, quotas, cost review, and access control. Which GCP boundary is usually the first conversation?

options.text: Separate projects for the environments, with labels and folder placement reviewed.
One bucket prefix per environment, because prefixes replace project boundaries.
One service account for every human and runtime.

questions.explanation: Projects are the everyday boundary for resources, IAM, quotas, logs, APIs, and billing linkage. Separate projects are often the first environment-separation discussion.

questions.scenario: Which placement facts belong in the production project record?

options.text: Folder or organization context where inherited policy may come from.
Primary region and main service families.
Only the name of the last engineer who deployed.

questions.explanation: Project identity, hierarchy context, location, and service intent make the workload findable and reviewable. A last deployer is not the workload home.
